question: Which city was ranked as the world's most liveable city in the Global Liveability Index 2026?
detail:
- The **Economist Intelligence Unit (EIU)** released the **Global Liveability Index 2026**, comparing **173 cities** on where it is best to live.
- **Copenhagen (Denmark)** was the world's **Most Liveable City** for the **second year in a row**, followed by **Vienna (Austria)** second and **Melbourne (Australia)** third.
- **New Delhi** ranked **120th** of 173. Other Indian cities: **Mumbai (121st)**, **Chennai (123rd)** and **Bengaluru (127th)**.
- **About the Index:** an annual report measuring quality of life in the world's cities. Cities are assessed on more than **30 indicators**.
- **About the EIU:** a global research and analysis organisation that produces economic, political and social reports on the world's countries and cities. Established **1946**; headquarters **London (UK)**; parent organisation — **The Economist Group**.

question: Who won the Wimbledon 2026 Men's Singles title?
detail:
- **Jannik Sinner** of **Italy** won the **Wimbledon 2026 Men's Singles** title, beating **Alexander Zverev** of Germany in the final.
- It is his **second Wimbledon title in a row** and the **fifth Grand Slam** of his career.
- In the **Women's Singles**, **Linda Nosková** of the Czech Republic beat **Karolína Muchová** to win her **first Grand Slam** title.
- **Wimbledon 2025 for reference:** Gentlemen's Singles — winner **Jannik Sinner** (Italy), runner-up **Carlos Alcaraz** (Spain); Ladies' Singles — winner **Iga Swiatek** (Poland), runner-up **Amanda Anisimova** (USA).
- **About the Championship:** the world's oldest and most prestigious Grand Slam tennis tournament. Founded **1877**. It is the **only Grand Slam played on grass court**.

question: Famous playback singer S. Janaki passed away recently. Which civilian honour did she refuse to accept in the year 2013?
detail:
- Famous playback singer **S. Janaki** passed away at the age of **88** in **Mysuru (Karnataka)**. She was known as the **"Nightingale of South India"**.
- She contributed to Indian film music for nearly **70 years** and sang more than **48,000 songs** in many Indian languages.
- In **2013** she **refused to accept the Padma Bhushan**. She felt that, given her long musical journey, she should have been given the **Bharat Ratna**.
- **About S. Janaki:** from **Andhra Pradesh**; one of India's most famous playback singers; sang around **40,000+ songs in 17+ languages**; won **4 National Film Awards**.
- **Note:** Sahitya Akademi Award-winning Tamil writer **Poomani** also passed away recently.

question: Where was a Guinness World Record created by planting over 3.61 lakh saplings in one hour using the Miyawaki Method?
detail:
- A **Guinness World Record** was set in **Gandhinagar, Gujarat** by planting more than **3.61 lakh saplings in one hour** using the **Miyawaki Method**.
- The drive aimed to increase green cover, fight climate change and promote environmental conservation.
- The record was made with the participation of the **Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation (AMC)**, more than **25,000 volunteers** and local people.
- The plantation drive was held under the **'Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam'** campaign.
- **What is the Miyawaki Method:** a Japanese technique for growing a dense, fast-growing forest in very little space. It was developed by the Japanese botanist **Dr. Akira Miyawaki**.
- **How it works:** only **native** plant species are used; saplings are planted at very **high density** in a small area; the soil is improved with **organic compost**; and the plants are cared for well for the first **2–3 years**.

question: When is World Youth Skills Day celebrated?
detail:
- **World Youth Skills Day** is celebrated every year on **15 July**.
- The day stresses teaching young people the skills of **employment and entrepreneurship**.
- It was started by the **UN General Assembly** in **2014**, and first celebrated on **15 July 2015**.
- This year marks its **11th anniversary**.
- **2026 Theme:** "Skills for a Shared Future".

question: Which virus has been identified for the first time in India in Psittacine birds?
detail:
- Scientists at the **Assam Veterinary and Fishery University (Guwahati)** identified the **Parrot Bornavirus-4 (PaBV-4)** virus in India for the **first time**, and carried out its **genetic characterisation**.
- **What is PaBV-4:** a virus that infects parrots and birds of the parrot family (**Psittacine birds**). It causes a serious disease in them called **Proventricular Dilatation Disease (PDD)**, which affects their **digestive** and **nervous** systems.
- **Psittacine birds** are birds belonging to the parrot family — parrot, parakeet, macaw, cockatoo, cockatiel, lovebird, budgerigar.
- **About AVFU:** a state university of the Assam government, set up for study, research and training in veterinary science, animal husbandry and fishery science. Established **2024**; located at **Khanapara, Guwahati, Assam**.

question: The statue of which famous social reformer and freedom fighter was unveiled in New Delhi by the Vice President of India?
detail:
- Vice President **C. P. Radhakrishnan** unveiled the statue of social reformer and freedom fighter **Mannathu Padmanabhan** at the **Mannam International Centre** in New Delhi, and inaugurated the **Mannam Smrithi Mandapam**.
- The event was organised by the **Nair Service Society (NSS)**.
- **About Mannathu Padmanabhan:** a famous social reformer, freedom fighter from Kerala and the founder of the **Nair Service Society (NSS)**. He worked for education, social equality and equal rights. He founded the NSS in **1914**, and inspired by Mahatma Gandhi he led the **Savarna Jatha**.
- **Major movement — Vaikom Satyagraha (1924–25):** India's **first big anti-untouchability movement**, aimed at winning everyone the equal right to walk on the public roads around the **Vaikom Mahadeva Temple**.
- **Honours:** **Padma Bhushan (1966)**; he was also given the title **"Bharata Kesari"**.
- **About the NSS:** a social organisation of Kerala founded by Mannathu Padmanabhan on **31 October 1914**. Headquarters — **Kottayam, Kerala**.

question: Where was the 1st ICAI Global Orbit Summit 2026 held?
detail:
- The **Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI)** inaugurated the first **ICAI Global Orbit Summit 2026** in **New Delhi**.
- The summit aimed to bring Chartered Accountants from India and around the world, along with industry, government and financial experts, onto one platform to discuss new subjects in the global economy, accounting, auditing, taxation, **Artificial Intelligence (AI)** and finance.
- Its theme was **"ICAI Global Orbit: From GCCs to Global Leadership"**.
- The summit series will next be held in **Ahmedabad, Mumbai, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Pune and Chennai**.
- **About ICAI:** a statutory body of India that runs and regulates the education, examination, training and profession of Chartered Accountants. Established **1949** under the **Chartered Accountants Act, 1949**; headquarters **New Delhi**; President (2026) — **CA. Prasanna Kumar D.**

question: What is the new name of the 'Central Vista' redevelopment area?
detail:
- The central government has renamed the **Central Vista Redevelopment Area** to the **Kartavya Bhawan Complex**.
- This new name will now be used in place of Central Vista in all official documents and government references.
- The new complex includes **Kartavya Path**, the **Kartavya Bhawans** and all government buildings to be built there in future.
- Its main aim is to bring the various Union Ministries into one modern administrative complex under the **Common Central Secretariat**.
- **About the project:** Central Vista is New Delhi's central administrative area, where the main offices of the Government of India are located. The project's aim is to build modern government buildings and bring the ministries into a single complex. It connects **Rashtrapati Bhavan to India Gate**.
- **Related renamings:** Rajpath → **Kartavya Path (2022)** — the roughly **2 km** road connecting Rashtrapati Bhavan to India Gate; New Prime Minister's Office Complex → **Seva Teerth (2025)**; Central Vista → **Kartavya Bhawan Complex (2026)**.

question: Which organisation is preparing India's first Indian Standard for the restoration of Mangrove Ecosystems?
options: Indian Council of Forestry Research and Education (ICFRE)
detail:
- The **Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS)** is preparing an **Indian Standard** for the **restoration of mangrove ecosystems** — the first time this is being done in India.
- The draft standard is named **"Restoration of Mangrove Ecosystem – Guidelines"**. Its aim is to create uniform, scientific guidelines for mangrove restoration across the country.
- **About mangroves:** plants that grow in the **saline water and tidal areas** of coastal regions. They play an important role in preventing **coastal erosion**, protecting marine **biodiversity** and shielding coastal areas from natural disasters.
- **Major mangrove areas in India:** **Sundarbans (West Bengal)**, Gujarat, Odisha, Andhra Pradesh, Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu and the Andaman & Nicobar Islands. The **Sundarbans** is India's largest mangrove forest.
- **MISHTI Programme:** **Mangrove Initiative for Shoreline Habitats and Tangible Incomes**, started in **2023** for the conservation and expansion of mangrove forests. Target — **540 sq. km** of mangrove restoration; covers **9 coastal states & 4 union territories**; extended till **2029**; total allocation **₹600 crore**.
